solutions.solution:
(D) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship
(a) (M + K – T) = Rejected
M is the maternal aunt of T
(b) (M × K – T) = Selected
M is the Maternal uncle of T
Hence, there is no need to check other options.

(C) By applying above decoding method for (P +R + S + Q), we draw the following family diagram
R is Daughter, P is Sister, S is Father
P is the aunt of Q
Hence, P is the aunt of Q.

(D) By applying above decoding method for (P – R + Q), we draw the following family diagram
P is Married couple, R is Father
P is the mother of Q
Hence, P is the mother of Q.

(C) By applying above decoding method for (P × R + Q), we draw the following family diagram
P is the son of Q
Daughter, Sister
Hence, P is the son of Q.

(C) By applying above decoding method for (P × R – Q), we get draw the following family diagram
Married couple, Brother
P is the brother-in-law of Q
Therefore, P is the brother-in-law of Q.

(C) By applying above decoding method for (P + R + Q), we draw the following family diagram
Married couple
Father
Therefore, P is the husband of Q.

(C) By applying above decoding method (P – R × Q), we draw the following family diagram
Married couple, Brother
P is sister-in-law of Q
Therefore, P is sister-in-law of Q.

(D) N × K – M + L, according to the given information,
N is Brother, K is Son, M is Sister, L
From the figure above, it is clear that L is the daughter of K and niece of uncle N.

(C) The best method to follow is the option method.
We have to check which option represents M is the grandfather of T.
For M to be the grandfather of T, we have to see the given statements i.e., if P – Q means Q is the father of P.
We need two (–) symbols make the correct answer but none of the options has two symbols (–).
